source: PoE Wiki with official patch references and official forum links

Definition.text: Trigger is a skill tag for mechanics that cause supported/owned skills to cast automatically when their trigger condition is met.

Purpose.text: Attach automatic-cast behavior to another skill source without a direct cast action for the triggered skill.

Core Rules.text: Triggers are event-driven: support gems or item modifiers evaluate conditions such as critical hit, kill, or on-hit and attempt to cast target skills automatically.

Internal Rules.text: Trigger events can require a cooldown check and are constrained by trigger-specific cooldown groups and direct cast restrictions depending on source and patch state.

Calculation Order.text: 1) condition met on source skill event, 2) evaluate trigger availability/cooldown/state, 3) enqueue triggered skill execution according to trigger ordering, 4) apply cast costs and reservation modifiers.

Priority Rules.text: For patched behavior where multiple trigger mechanics interact, a single trigger group shares cooldown between gem versions of the same triggered action.

Preconditions.text: A valid triggering source, a compatible triggered skill, and supported condition must exist; unsupported origins (totem/trap/mine/Vaal/channelling/reservation where disallowed) cannot trigger.

Postconditions.text: Triggered skill is cast under trigger item/gem restrictions; some rules may forbid direct player cast of the same triggered skill in same state.

Limitations.text: A trigger source may be limited to one skill per trigger event and cooldown-group restrictions apply in current and later patch states.

Caps.text: Cooldown limits and one-skill-per-event limit cap burst density regardless of number of eligible trigger sources.

Formula.text: No standalone numeric scalar; operational rule is event-to-trigger and cooldown/ordering gating per trigger event.

Conflicts.text: Trigger systems are separate from Cast; Trigger uses automatic condition-based casting rules, not direct player activation.

Exceptions.text: Certain minion trigger items and historical cases may keep exception behavior even when general trigger restrictions are applied.

Hidden Rules.text: Version transitions include explicit compatibility and disallowance decisions that are not always obvious from one-line tooltip summaries.

Developer Notes.text: Patch 3.21.0 aligned item-trigger and gem-trigger cooldown grouping for the same action to prevent duplicate firing.

Historical Changes.text: Introduction at patch 1.0.0; single-trigger-gem restriction in 1.2.2; item/gem costs and trigger cooldown behavior changes through 2.4.0, 3.15.0, and 3.21.0.

text.note: Added Trigger gems for automatic casting on conditions.
Restricted skills to work with only one trigger gem at a time.
Trigger skill gems now have mana costs; item trigger modifiers gained standardized reservation behavior unless otherwise specified.
Any Trigger Skills from skill gems now share cooldown groups with corresponding gem versions.
Cast on Critical Strike and Cast on Melee Kill explicitly documented to trigger one skill per trigger event with per-triggered-skill cooldown.

text: Cast on Crit Support creates an automatic cast path from attack critical event to linked spell.
CoC-like trigger event with cooldown can prevent immediate re-trigger of same action until group cooldown resolves.
Trigger is not equal to Cast
Trigger is not guaranteed for every qualifying hit unless cooldown and conditions are met
Can does not imply Trigger unless explicit condition and valid trigger source exist
